verdicts.note: Score and citations are sound, but the scoring-file highlight 'Endorsed by Gov. Ferguson, Rep. Schrier, WA Labor Council, WA Education Association, and Eastside Firefighters [S2]' miscites: per the dossier, Schrier, WA Labor Council, WEA, and Eastside Firefighters are pamphlet endorsements [S1]; only Ferguson (and other additions) come from the campaign site [S2].

The entire score rests on a single Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates endorsement listed in the pamphlet [S1]. The dossier contains no candidate statement on reproductive rights, LGBTQ+ issues, or curricula anywhere. One endorsement is not a 'strong endorsement pattern' under the confidence rules; this is an indirect inference and belongs at low, not medium.

Direction is right, but 'high' is overstated. The only explicit policy statement — police 'have been left without clear tools to protect victims and neighborhoods' — appears solely in the pamphlet [S1]; S2 supports only her career biography. A 35-year law-enforcement career is a resume, not a policy record on the enforcement-vs-prevention question (her documented programs are anti-bullying curricula and community coalitions). A single pamphlet phrase plus occupational inference is medium at best.

The 'accountability' language in the dossier [S1][S2] is aimed at politicians focused on 'reelection campaigns, political outrage, and national talking points' — a governance-process critique, not a fiscal audit-and-restrain position. Nothing in the dossier addresses spending levels, audits, or restraint before new spending, and he simultaneously calls for improved public-safety and mental-health services [S2], which cuts the other way. The lean is an indirect inference; confidence must drop to low.
